title: Annamalai submits resignation letter to BJP chief; to launch new party in 6-8 months
content: Former Tamil Nadu BJP president K Annamalai met with party leaders in Delhi, submitting his resignation amid speculation of launching a new regional party.  Associates suggest the outfit will have a "secular" and "Tamil-first" outlook, aiming to be an alternative to existing political forces in the state.

title: '15 lakh hits within 2 mins': CBSE re-evaluation portal faces massive cyberattack
content: CBSE reported that its re-evaluation portal faced multiple cyberattack attempts while processing over 16,000 student submissions for verification and re-evaluation of Class 12 answer sheets. The board said a denial-of-service attack generated nearly 1.5 million hits within two minutes, along with over one lakh unauthorized access attempts. Despite the disruptions, the platform remained operational. The portal, delayed for infrastructure upgrades, will stay open until June 6 with extended session limits and improved stability for applicants.

title: How Sooryavanshi combined Kohli & Gayle to produce greatest IPL season
content: If the question is which batter produced the most extreme combination of volume, speed and power across a single IPL season, the evidence points overwhelmingly in one direction. The IPL has seen bigger run tallies. It has seen cleaner six-hitters. What it had never seen before 2026 was a player score nearly 800 runs while striking at 237 and smashing 72 sixes. For 18 seasons, volume and violence existed separately. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi merged them, and that is why his IPL campaign 2026 may be one of the greatest batting seasons ever.

title: 'Trump effect' comes to IBM stock, 6 months after Trump called IBM CEO a legend
content: IBM shares have seen a significant surge, adding billions to its market value, following a resurfaced video of Donald Trump praising CEO Arvind Krishna. This renewed attention, coupled with optimism around AI and quantum computing developments, has fueled a rally, with analysts pointing to social media buzz and momentum trading as key drivers.

title: Last-minute parleys? Karnataka CM-designate DKS, Sidda meet Rahul & Kharge
content: Congress leaders met in Delhi to finalise the Karnataka Cabinet. DK Shivakumar and Siddaramaiah discussed ministerial berths and deputy chief minister posts. The swearing-in ceremony for the new Chief Minister is scheduled for Wednesday. The party aims to form a stable government. Discussions are ongoing to finalize the complete list of ministers.

title: From Messi to Ronaldo, 13 legends who could play their final FIFA World Cup
content: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is expected to serve as a farewell stage for several of football’s most iconic players. From Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo to Luka Modrić, Neymar and Mohamed Salah, a generation that has defined the modern game could be making its final World Cup appearances. For some, it is one last chance at glory; for others, a final opportunity to cement enduring legacies.

title: Google plans to release 32mn Wolbachia-infected mosquitoes across Florida, California
content: Alphabet's Debug initiative is seeking EPA approval to release 32 million specially treated male mosquitoes in California and Florida. This innovative approach uses Wolbachia bacteria to sterilize wild Culex mosquitoes, aiming to significantly reduce the spread of diseases like West Nile virus. Advanced AI and robotics are enabling this large-scale effort, with trials planned over two years.

title: 'A lot of Indians have struggled': Manjrekar bats for more foreign captains in IPL
content: Former India cricketer Sanjay Manjrekar has advocated for more foreign captains in the IPL, saying teams should select the best leader regardless of nationality. While acknowledging that Indian captains boost a franchise’s brand value and fan appeal, he argued that leadership quality should take precedence. Manjrekar cited successful overseas skippers like David Warner and Pat Cummins as examples.

title: Microsoft tells engineers to stop using Anthropic's Claude
content: Microsoft is discontinuing most internal Claude Code licenses by June 30, directing engineers to its own GitHub Copilot CLI. This move follows Claude Code's unexpected popularity, which reportedly undercut Microsoft's homegrown product. While officially citing toolchain unification, the timing suggests cost-cutting amidst an industry-wide AI spending crunch.

title: Mike Vrabel-Dianna Russini scandal resurfaces after photo claim
content: The Patriots' blockbuster acquisition of A.J. Brown quickly became overshadowed by controversy after a viral social media claim linked head coach Mike Vrabel to former NFL insider Dianna Russini. The allegation gained significant attention despite no evidence supporting it. Meanwhile, Brown's move from Philadelphia to New England has sparked optimism about the Patriots' offense, with former NFL star Chris Long believing the receiver can help maximize quarterback Drake Maye's development and accelerate the team's Super Bowl ambitions.

title: Wife loses mobile number after husband's 'bogus complaint', Allahabad HC slams cyber cell
content: The Allahabad High Court has taken a firm stance against the Cyber Cell in Lucknow, chastising them for their automated decision to suspend a woman's mobile number amidst a marital dispute. The judges criticized the lack of thoughtful consideration in the authorities' approach, viewing it as a misuse of state resources.

title: Kelce caught in fresh Blake Lively–Taylor Swift drama rumors
content: Taylor Swift’s upcoming wedding to Travis Kelce has sparked renewed attention on some of her high-profile friendships. Reports claim Swift has grown distant from former close friend Karlie Kloss, while her relationship with Blake Lively remains under scrutiny following the actress’s legal dispute with Justin Baldoni. Sources suggest Swift has become more selective about her inner circle, prioritizing trust and long-term loyalty as she prepares for the next chapter of her life with Kelce.

title: Cowboys, Eagles Eye Blockbuster move for $160M Defensive Standout
content: The Dallas Cowboys and Philadelphia Eagles were among the teams linked to former Cleveland Browns star Myles Garrett before the Los Angeles Rams completed a blockbuster trade. Cleveland received rising pass rusher Jared Verse, a 2027 first-round pick, and additional draft assets. Garrett, whose contract is worth roughly $160 million, is expected to receive a new deal in Los Angeles as the Rams strengthen their championship hopes.

title: 'Kar diya na maine': Rajat Patidar's first words after RCB's title win
content: Moments after RCB’s IPL 2026 victory, Patidar shared an emotional embrace with childhood friend Abhishek Pathrod, saying, “Dekha, jeet gaye. Kar diya na maine." The triumph capped a remarkable rise for the soft-spoken leader, whose calm captaincy and prolific batting helped transform RCB from perennial underachievers into back-to-back champions.

title: Kala Hiran director reacts to Salman Khan legal notice
content: The controversy around Kala Hiran has intensified after reports claimed Salman Khan’s legal team issued a notice over alleged similarities to the blackbuck poaching case. Director Bharat S. Shrinate said he is used to legal pressure, citing Udaipur Files. Producer Amit Jani alleged intimidation, while Salman’s team has not responded. The film is reportedly based on the 1998 case.

title: NMC's proposed 10-year MBBS limit: Why experts say student support matters more than one extra year
content: The National Medical Commission's proposal to restore the maximum duration for completing MBBS from nine years to 10 years has reignited debate on student welfare and academic standards. Senior medical educator Dr. M. M. A. Faridi argues that the focus should be on counselling, mentoring and identifying the reasons behind academic struggles rather than the one-year difference. Meanwhile, United Doctors Front President Dr. Babita Sikriwal supports the proposal, calling for stronger student support systems and reconsideration of the four-attempt First Professional MBBS rule.

title: Twisha Sharma death case: Giribala Singh, Samarth sent to jail under judicial custody
content: A special CBI court in Bhopal is expected to send Giribala Singh, a former district judge, and her son Samarth Singh to judicial custody today. Both are accused in the dowry death case of model-turned-actor Twisha Sharma. The CBI did not seek further remand for the duo. The court's decision is anticipated this afternoon.

title: Delhi man spends Rs 14,000 flying to Hyderabad for interview, rejected in 10 mins
content: A user on the r/delhi forum of Reddit shared the incident, saying he works in industrial fermentation and had been shortlisted for a fermentation-related role at a “major pharma company” in Hyderabad.

title: Debt, inflation & IMF dependence: Pakistan still spending $900k every month on US lobbying
content: Pakistan is spending heavily on lobbying in the United States. This occurs as the nation faces economic difficulties and water scarcity. The country also deals with rising fuel costs. FARA filings reveal extensive efforts to influence Washington. This includes security, trade, and diplomatic outreach. The lobbying intensified during military tensions with India. Pakistan's army chief made claims about mediation.

title: Court awards Rs 2.5 lakh compensation as bank loses original land title document
content: A district consumer commission has ordered the Central Bank of India to pay ₹2.5 lakh compensation for losing a crucial land title document deposited as security for a loan. The bank's negligence prevented the property owner from fully exercising her rights, impacting her ability to secure further loans and realize the property's full value.

title: 'Isolate yourself': Govt advisory for travellers from Ebola-hit countries showing symptoms
content: India's Health Ministry has issued a travel advisory for those arriving from Ebola-hit nations, assuring no cases are currently reported domestically. Travelers from affected regions within the last 21 days experiencing symptoms like fever, vomiting, or bleeding are urged to self-isolate and contact health authorities immediately via the 24x7 helpline 1075 for guidance.

title: West Bengal BJP shuts doors on TMC rebels, vows to prevent 'Trinamoolisation'
content: Samik Bhattacharya, the West Bengal BJP leader, has taken a definitive stand against former TMC leaders who wish to join their ranks, branding them as 'tainted' and insisting that the BJP thrives on grassroots support. He also dismissed Mamata Banerjee's intentions to stage a protest over supposed post-poll violence as an attempt to sidestep her party's internal challenges.

title: 'Would not be a great idea': Manjrekar on sending Sooryavanshi overseas too soon
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's explosive IPL 2026 performance, including 776 runs and 72 sixes, has ignited calls for his immediate promotion to India's T20I squad. While many former cricketers back the young talent, ex-player Sanjay Manjrekar urges caution, advocating for gradual exposure to international conditions and allowing his career to develop organically.

title: Shahid Kapoor-Kriti Sanon chemistry wins hearts; Rashmika Mandanna adds to chaos
content: The trailer for Homi Adajania's 'Cocktail 2', starring Shahid Kapoor, Kriti Sanon, and Rashmika Mandanna, has been released, generating significant buzz. The romantic comedy promises a story filled with relationships, friendship, and complicated situations, with fans praising the performances and music. The film is set to hit theaters on June 19.

title: ED cracks down on Dawood aide in Maharashtra, Gujarat raids
content: Enforcement Directorate conducted extensive raids across Maharashtra and Gujarat, targeting over 20 locations in a money laundering probe linked to drug trafficker Mohammad Salim Dola. Dola, recently extradited from Türkiye, is accused of running a transnational drug syndicate and is a close associate of Dawood Ibrahim.

title: Iran war impact: RBI likely sold $12bn gold reserves to shield foreign currency assets
content: To limit the impact of these external shocks, the government has stepped up measures aimed at reducing foreign exchange outflows and stabilising the economy. These steps include increases in fuel prices and a sharp rise in import duties on precious metals.

title: Virat Kohli shares 'Kikli' dance with Bhuvneshwar Kumar after RCB triumph - Watch
content: Virat Kohli and Bhuvneshwar Kumar celebrated Royal Challengers Bengaluru's second consecutive IPL title with a hilarious "Kikli" dance. Both veteran players were instrumental in RCB's dominant campaign, with Kohli leading the batting and Bhuvneshwar spearheading the bowling attack to secure their back-to-back championship wins.

title: Why Washington is considering expanding nuclear deployments across Europe
content: America is considering expanding its nuclear weapons deployment to more NATO allies, with eastern European nations like Poland reportedly showing interest. This move aims to bolster deterrence amid rising tensions with Russia. These arrangements, historically central to NATO's defense, ensure allies share in nuclear policy and risk, strengthening collective security and the US commitment to protect its partners.

title: “Koi mat aana yaha pe” Kedarnath Dham pilgrim shares her harrowing experience
content: A recent Kedarnath Yatra pilgrimage highlights significant challenges faced by travellers, including severe traffic jams and overcrowded routes. Despite extensive planning, one pilgrim's journey became a test of endurance, culminating in an inability to enter the temple due to overwhelming crowds, raising concerns about managing high tourist traffic at popular Indian pilgrimage sites.

title: India moves closer to mega Rafale deal, but will it solve the IAF's fighter shortage?
content: India is set to procure 114 Rafale fighter jets from France in a significant government-to-government deal worth Rs 3.25 lakh crore. While 18 will be fly-away, 96 will be manufactured in India. This acquisition aims to bolster the Indian Air Force's depleted squadron strength, though indigenous programs like Tejas Mk2 and AMCA are crucial for long-term security against evolving threats.

title: Hardik Pandya to reach BCCI CoE on June 2 ahead of Afghanistan ODI's
content: Hardik Pandya is set to undergo fitness drills at the BCCI Centre of Excellence in Bengaluru from June 2, aiming for the ODI squad against Afghanistan. Meanwhile, the Test team begins training in Mullanpur for their one-off match. Auqib Nabi might be a late addition to the Test camp.

title: Rs 25 lakh theft at Raveena Tandon's brother's house; caretaker arrested
content: In a dramatic turn of events, Rashi Chhabria, a caregiver, has found herself in handcuffs for supposedly stealing jewellery worth ₹25 lakh from the home of Raveena Tandon’s brother in Mumbai. Entrusted with the care of the star's mother, Chhabria had cultivated the family's trust before the precious items disappeared from a secure location.

title: 'Mental torture': Punjab student dies by suicide; alleges harassment over unpaid fees
content: A 17-year-old student tragically ended her life after allegedly enduring severe mental harassment from her school over ₹20,000 in unpaid fees. Before her death, she recorded a viral video statement from her hospital bed, detailing the ordeal and holding the school administration responsible. Police have registered a case and are actively searching for the accused.

title: H-1B hiring boost: Nvidia offers top salaries despite slowdown in foreign recruitment
content: While many tech firms scale back, Nvidia is aggressively hiring foreign talent via the H-1B visa program, increasing its certified positions. This surge reflects the company's immense demand for AI specialists and engineers, contrasting with competitors like Google and Amazon. CEO Jensen Huang emphasizes immigration's vital role in US technological leadership.

title: FEMA case: ED conducts searches against Vedanta Group
content: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) has initiated searches against the Vedanta Group as part of a Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA) investigation. The action against the mining conglomerate, promoted by Anil Agarwal, commenced on Monday. Further details regarding the ongoing FEMA probe are yet to be disclosed by the central agency.

title: Lalit Modi backs Sooryavanshi to become 'bigger than any Hollywood and Bollywood star'
content: Lalit Modi has predicted a meteoric rise for 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, who was the leading run-scorer in IPL 2026. Modi believes the Rajasthan Royals prodigy could score a T20 double century and become a global superstar, surpassing even Hollywood and Bollywood actors.

title: Kerala takes up coordinated pre-monsoon preparedness
content: Cleaning and monsoon preparedness measures were being carried out by the health and sanitation committees in 17,661 wards, Minister for Local Self-Governments K.M. Shaji informs the Assembly.

title: Australian teacher went missing in Amritsar; daughter seeks Punjab CM's help
content: “I seek help to bring back my father. My father has been missing since May 22 in Amritsar. We have no whereabouts for him yet. His phone is switched off,” says  Surbhi Sharma

title: Kanataka CM swearing-in ceremony: Elaborate security in place at Lok Bhavan in Bengaluru
content: Given the limited seating capacity at Glass House in Lok Bhavan, entry has been restricted strictly to invited guests and passes have already been issued to VIPs, VVIPs and other categorised invitees.

title: Tigress Zeenat gives birth to four cubs in Similipal
content: Forest Department takes special measures to ensure safety of mother and cubs, and their movements are being continuously monitored, says Odisha CM Mohan Majhi

title: ABVP flags lapses in entrance exams, seeks stronger implementation of reforms
content: ABVP blamed administrative failures, inadequate security safeguards, weak infrastructure and poor accountability for the lapses

title: Gujarat ACB arrests senior energy official on bribery charges
content: The Anti-Corruption Bureau arrested A.B. Chaudhary, a Class-I officer serving as Chief Electrical Inspector at Udyog Bhavan in Gandhinagar, and recovered assets worth more than ₹2.64 crore

title: Cub deaths prompt preventive measures for Asiatic lions in Bhavnagar
content: Steps include extensive health-monitoring exercise, including deworming, de-ticking and intensified field surveillance, officials say

title: No role for any third parties in resolution of India-Nepal border issue: MEA
content: External Affairs Ministry spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said both sides have established bilateral mechanisms to deal with all aspects of the border issue and that close to 98% of the India-Nepal boundary has been demarcated

title: Senior Congress leader P. Chidambaram meets Tamil Nadu CM Vijay
content: In a separate development, Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly Secretary K. Srinivasan resigned from his post.

title: Nepal’s ruling RSP chief meets BJP president Nitin Nabin; inquires about party work
content: The RSP delegation, led by Mr. Lamichhane, is on a visit to India following an invitation by BJP chief Mr. Nabin; it arrived in New Delhi on Monday, June 1 afternoon.

title: UGC qualification norms may cost 859 self-financing teachers their jobs in Kerala’s Kannur University
content: Vice-Chancellor insists on enforcing the regulations even as concerns are raised over the move affecting admissions and creating a crisis in higher education

title: EAM Jaishankar meets South African Deputy President Mashatile; trade, infra take centre stage
content: The South African Deputy President also met Vice-President C.P. Radhakrishnan and discussed various aspects of historical, cultural and people-to-people relations between the two nations

title: Visakhapatnam Beach Road set for upgrades ahead of Bhogapuram airport opening
content: Police plan to axe speed breakers, step up patrolling on the Beach Road route to Bhogapuram airport, and the CCTV network could be strengthened under the SARTHI project

title: Infections, fractures increasingly driving repeat hip replacement surgeries, finds study
content: It reflects the large number of young patients with demanding lifestyles who undergo these surgeries and subsequently experience complications.

title: CBI arrests two accused in Uttarakhand LUCC chit fund case
content: Over one lakh people in Uttarakhand were lured into making investments, totalling about ₹800 crore, in various unregulated deposit schemes of the Loni Urban Multi State Credit and Thrift Co-operative Society, the agency alleges

title: Maharashtra Cabinet clears farm loan waiver; 56 lakh farmers to benefit
content: The scheme will cover more than 65 lakh accounts, and loans worth ₹36,585 crore of nearly 56 lakh farmers are expected to be waived

title: Majority of TMC MLAs will remain with Mamata: Sobhandeb amid split speculations
content: While there has been no official confirmation, speculations suggest the number of legislators in touch with the rebel camp could range between 20 and 50

title: Traffic challans in Telangana to be sent through SMS, email and WhatsApp
content: All vehicle owners are required to update their mobile phone numbers and e-mail addresses on the VAHAN portal within one month from the date of publication of the notification

title: Fazil Nagar town to be renamed as Pawagarh says U.P. CM Yogi Adityanath
content: The proposed name ‘Pawagarh’ is derived from Pawapuri in Bihar, a revered Jain pilgrimage site closely associated with the Jain Tirthankar Lord Mahavir

title: ED arrests promoters of Earth Infrastructures Ltd. in home buyer fraud case
content: The agency alleges the group collected ₹2,004 crore from more than 19,425 home buyers and promised timely deliver, but ₹467 crore was siphoned off through related entities and persons

title: Congress slams RSS linked ‘task forces’ for PESA, FRA implementation in MP, Chhattisgarh
content: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh alleged that the essential character of Panchayat (Extension to Scheduled Areas) Act, 1996 and the Forest Rights Act, 2006 in letter and spirit is being deliberately sabotaged by such task forces which have executive responsibilities as well.

title: No dustbin, no business: Kurnool civic chief fines two shops on sanitation round
content: A surprise check turns up a staff shortage and poor upkeep at the Collectorate’s Anna Canteen during the Kurnool commissioner’s sanitation round

title: In Odisha, one in five women face domestic violence; one in four children stunted: NFHS-6
content: One in five women face partner violence and one in four children are stunted in Odisha, according to the latest NFHS-6 survey, which also flags low female economic participation and a steep rise in C-section deliveries as key concerns for the State

title: NEET paper leak: Delhi court extends judicial custody of five accused till June 15
content: On June 1, physics lecturer Manisha Sanjay Hawaldar, paediatrician Dr. Manoj Shirure and physics teacher Tejas Harshadkumar Shah were sent to 14 days of judicial custody

title: Five new Supreme Court judges sworn in, strength increases to 37
content: The appointments come shortly after the Union government increased the court’s sanctioned strength from 33 to 37 judges, excluding the CJI; the latest round of elevations is also expected to balance regional representation and enhance gender diversity on the Bench

title: Kerala BJP MLA faces backlash for dropping toffees on desks for students at school event
content: Video of V. Muraleedharan sprinkling toffees on desks instead of handing them directly to students during a school reopening function has gone viral

title: CPI(M) would have avoided its current situation in Kerala if it understood what Lenin meant: G. Sudhakaran
content: Veteran leader recalls famous text One Step Forward, Two Steps Back: The Crisis in Our Party, written by Vladimir Lenin, in the Assembly; he says the party should have listened to the voices of the people of Kerala

title: Karnataka legislature signs MoU to develop National e-Vidhan app, digitise operations
content: The aim is to digitise operations of Karnataka Legislative Assembly and Karnataka Legislative Council

title: Kandula Durgesh to receive Best Tourism Promotion State award for AP at GTM Expo
content: Tourism Minister Kandula Durgesh visits Kerala on June 3 to 5 for the GTM Expo 2026 and receives the award at Thiruvananthapuram on June 4

title: Return to Uddhav Thackeray's Sena if you regret your mistake, says Sanjay Raut to Shinde faction leaders
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Raut said that the BJP was never the "elder brother" in the original saffron alliance in Maharashtra

title: 220 MLAs in Tamil Nadu secured less than 50% of votes in their constituencies: ADR report
content: According to the ADR report, the winners of the Tamil Nadu Assembly election in 2026 won with an average of 38.99% of total votes polled. In the 2021 election, the winners won with an average of 48.37% polled, it added.

title: 2,700-year-old ‘Munivahana Seva’ replicated in Tirupati to promote caste equity
content: Government teachers carry a Dalit colleague into a Tirupati temple, re-enacting a 2,700-year-old episode linked to the Sri Vaishnavite saint Tiruppan Alwar

title: KGBV recruitment: Samagra Shiksha warns candidates against brokers for 299 posts
content: Samagra Shiksha says hiring for 299 KGBV posts is automated and merit-based; candidates told to report anyone demanding money

title: SC directs removal of encroachments from Agasthyamalai ecological landscape
content: The Supreme Court also directed that disciplinary and legal action be initiated against all identified 118 serving and retired government servants who are found to be encroachers

title: NSUI moves Delhi HC over CBSE’s On-Screen Marking system, seeks independent inquiry
content: NSUI petitions Delhi High Court for an independent inquiry into CBSE’s On-Screen Marking (OSM) system amid widespread student concerns

title: Advance on strength of education and build strong, self-reliant India: President Murmu to youth
content: President Droupadi Murmu said that the youth represent the strength, hope, and bright future of our nation

title: Trinamool chief Mamata sits on dharna in Kolkata to protest 'post-poll violence'
content: She was seen accompanied by the party's old guards Firhad Hakim, Madan Mitra, Derek O'Brien, Kalyan Banerjee and Dola Sen, amid the conspicuous absence of most of the fresh faces who won the Assembly seats on TMC tickets

title: Karnataka changes alcohol pricing | India’s first alcohol-based tax system explained
content: Karnataka has become the first state in the country to implement the Alcohol in Beverage (AIB) excise duty structure.

title: Two sisters charred to death in fire in Eluru district’s Agency area
content: A predawn fire guts two houses at Velerupadu in Eluru’s Agency area, killing two elderly sisters; the cause is not yet known

title: Self-styled spiritual guru held for allegedly drugging, raping and blackmailing women in Mathura
content: The case came to light after a 22-year-old BSc Nursing student from Chhattisgarh lodged a complaint at Govardhan police station on May 25

title: Srinagar airport to be shut for fortnight from October 1 for maintenance; locals fear blow to business
content: Starting in July, airport operations will be closed for two days every week, culminating in the full 15-day closure from October 1 to October 15

title: Youth Congress to intensify nationwide protests, demands removal of Education Minister Pradhan
content: Indian Youth Congress said it would launch a fresh phase of protests across several states, alleging that repeated examination controversies have undermined students' faith in the education system

title: Congress leader Anand Sharma meets visiting South African Deputy President
content: Paul Mashatile referred to the special and historic relations between India and South Africa and the inspiration both countries drew from Mahatma Gandhi and Nelson Mandela

title: IMD warns of heavy rain in Kerala; orange alerts issued as monsoon likely by June 4
content: Orange alert for three northern Kerala districts — Malappuram, Kozhikode and Wayanad — for Tuesday, and yellow alert for remaining 11 districts

title: Tiruchi nursing student death: Safety concerns raised over Dexamethasone injection batch
content: Following the adverse cardiac events, hospital authorities flagged concerns regarding the safety of the drug with health officials after which TNMSC has initiated testing of the drug batch

title: Twisha Sharma death case: Bhopal court sends Samarth and Giribala Singh to 14-day judicial custody
content: A day earlier, the CBI reconstructed the circumstances of the former model’s alleged suicide at her marital home here using dummies

title: Over 550 Indian peacekeepers in South Sudan awarded U.N. Medal of Honour
content: The peacekeepers were recognised for their efforts in ‘protecting civilians through patrols, community engagement, veterinary camps, women's self-defence training, combating gender violence and improving humanitarian access’

title: MDMK general council will decide party’s future course of action: Durai Vaiko
content: Speaking to The Hindu later, Mr. Durai Vaiko clarified that the party continued to be a part of the DMK alliance

title: Late Or Weak It May Be, But Monsoon Never Failed India In 13 Million Years
content: Continuity does not mean stability. Paleoclimate records reveal that the monsoon has gone through dramatic fluctuations in the past. One of the most significant events occurred around 4,200 years ago, when a major drought hit large parts of Asia.

title: Punjab Claims It Beat Kerala, Delhi To Claim Top Spot In School Education
content: The AAP government, which came to power in 2022, said the state has now secured the top position in the country, ahead of Kerala, Maharashtra and Delhi.

title: Venezuela Acting President To Visit India From June 3-7: Foreign Ministry
content: Addressing a briefing, MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said that the Venezuelan leader would be accompanied by a high-level delegation comprising several ministers.

title: 'Why Doesn't CBSE Make Him Adviser?' Panel After Student's Presentation
content: Sarthak Sidhant alleged that a comparison of multiple CBSE tender documents revealed several discrepancies that, according to him, appeared to favour a particular service provider

title: Putin's New Friends: Russia's Military Pact With Taliban Amid Ukraine War
content: Russia has signed a military cooperation agreement with the Taliban, further strengthening ties with Afghanistan's ruling group and reinforcing its position as the only country to officially recognise the Taliban government.

title: Russia Accuses Kyiv Of 'Acts Of Terror' After Attacks On Ukrainian Cities
content: Moscow has said it is stepping up its strikes on military targets in Ukraine in retaliation for what it said was a devastating Ukrainian drone strike.

title: A Chaotic Hearing In Twisha Sharma Case That Nearly Led To A Fistfight
content: The hearing today in Twisha Sharma case witnessed high drama, heated confrontations, and explosive allegations.

title: 'India Better Placed Amid Iran War Than In 2013': Ex-RBI Governor To NDTV
content: With the Iran war keeping crude elevated and battering currencies worldwide, former RBI Governor D Subbarao tells NDTV's Gaurie Dwivedi that India is no longer among the "fragile five" economies of 2013, and that the central bank should let the rupee find its own level.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Why Pete Hegseth's Unusual 'Shangri-La' Speech Should Worry India
content: Usually, the forum is not the stuff of headlines. This year, though, almost every country seemed uneasy, thanks to Hegseth.

title: Quack Flees After Leaving Pregnant UP Woman's Abdomen Open, Baby Survives
content: Following protests by family members, authorities found that the hospital was operating without the required permissions.

title: Dawood Still Operating From Karachi's Clifton Road: Delhi Police Probe
content: New Delhi, June 2 (IANS) The Special Cell of the Delhi Police busted a major terror module being run by the Dawood Ibrahim syndicate. The terror module was planning on carrying out attacks in Delhi and Mumbai, said the police following the arrests of

title: 'Raped By 600-700 Men': UK MP Shares 'Pak Grooming Gang' Testimonies
content: According to testimonies, gang members used racial comparisons to justify the way they "treated and controlled" women.

title: 'Era Of Emotional Purchases': 5 Business Ideas That Could Boom by 2030
content: According to entrepreneurs, mentors, and investors, the biggest opportunities may lie in solving specific problems for specific audiences.

title: Dehradun Woman Locked In Toilet For Months, Fed Raw Rice, Beaten By In-Laws
content: Her in-laws violently pulled out hair from her head, leaving her scalp bare, the woman's father alleged.

title: Adani Portfolio Reports Highest Ever Capex By Any Indian Corporate In FY26
content: Nearly 80 per cent of investments were directed towards core infrastructure platforms spanning energy, utilities, transport and logistics, according to India's leading infrastructure and utilities portfolio.

title: 51 Wounds, Cigarette Burns: Kerala Toddler Abused For A Month, Killed By Mother's Lover
content: On May 29, when the child cried loudly, the man struck him on the head, and destroyed evidence before taking the toddler to the hospital.

title: Drugs, Alchohol, Rave-Style Parties: Kolkata Cops Probe Double Death Case
content: Kolkata, June 2 (IANS) Police investigation into the Golf Green double death case has revealed that the flat, rented by Mohammad Dilshad, was frequently used for house parties involving young men and women, where drugs and alcohol were consumed, and

title: Bihar Law Aspirant, 17, Found Hanging In Hostel. Probe Underway
content: Patna, June 2 (IANS) A 17-year-old law entrance aspirant from Bihar's Samastipur district was found hanging from the ceiling fan in a hostel room in Patna's Secretariat Colony area on Tuesday, prompting a police investigation.

title: 'Aligning with traitor': Mamata attacks TMC rebels seeking to join Suvendu govt
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide.
